channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Colleges left helpless as students rule out schools due to state politics
item.description: Colleges and universities find themselves increasingly trapped with the politics of the state where they reside.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in shootings of Democratic office in Arizona had over 120 guns in his home, officials find
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — An Arizona prosecutor said the man arrested in the shooting of a Democratic National Committee office in suburban Phoenix had more than 120 guns and over 250,000 rounds of ammunition in his home, leading law enforcement to believe he may have been planning a mass casualty event.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW and WALTER BERRY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: France and other nations pledge massive aid package to support Lebanon at international conference
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France pledged to provide a 100-million euro ($108-million) package to support Lebanon at an international conference Thursday, as President Emmanuel Macron said “massive aid” is needed to support the country where war between Hezbollah militants and Israel has displaced a million peopl
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Should elephants have the same rights as people? A court in Colorado may decide
item.description: A court will hear arguments on whether the some elephants should be legally able to challenge their captivity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Should elephants have the same rights as people? A court in Colorado may decide
item.description: Colorado's highest court will hear arguments on whether the African female elephants should be legally able to challenge their captivity under a long-held process used by prisoners to dispute their detention.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Four memorable moments from Kamala Harris' CNN town hall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kamala Harris went to a Philadelphia suburb on Wednesday night for a CNN town hall, where she faced questions from undecided voters. The event was arranged after Donald Trump declined to participate in a second debate with her.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Boeing factory workers vote to reject contract and continue 6-week strike
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Boeing factory workers voted Wednesday to reject the company’s latest contract offer and to continue a six-week strike that has halted production of the aerospace giant’s bestselling jetliners.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG and MANUEL VALDES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The 2024 election is major source of stress: Survey
item.description: A total of 72% worry that the election results could lead to violence, and 56% believe the election could lead to the end of democracy in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Cash App founder's ex-wife slams alleged killer sister's testimony
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Bob Lee's ex-wife did not hold back her feelings toward the accused killer's sister at the courthouse on Wednesday. "My husband was murdered. She has no right to make herself a victim in this.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Wild wedding guest bites person's fingertip off after being told to leave Milwaukee nuptials
item.description: A Milwaukee man pleaded no contest before being found guilty of biting off the tip of a person's finger while attending a wedding in the Town of Oconomowoc in Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee AG exposes ICE's plan to release illegal immigrants convicted of dangerous crimes into state
item.description: Tennessee Attorney General Jonathan Skrmetti's office says ICE's plan to release migrants in the state was "derailed," and pushback from the governor and lawmakers halted the plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Goldfish changes name of iconic crackers to appeal to adults
item.description: Snack lovers can look forward to a playful twist on a childhood favorite, as Goldfish announces a limited-time renaming of its iconic Cheddar crackers.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspected car thief dead after high-speed chase, crash on live TV
item.description: A woman died after she was possibly ejected from the vehicle she was driving following a lengthy pursuit that ended in a high-speed crash on the 5 Freeway Wednesday afternoon. The pursuit began with the Los Angeles Police Department following an allegedly stolen Honda CR-V at about 1:45 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington judge upholds ban on at-home sexual assault kits
item.description: Leda Health challenged Washington's House Bill 1564, which was signed into law in 2023, and banned the sale and distribution of self-administered sexual assault kits.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: Pilot Chuck Coleman killed in air show crash in Las Cruces
item.description: Coleman was an aerobatic flight instructor for actors in the 2022 motion picture, "Top Gun: Maverick," where he flew more than 100 flights preparing actors to fly in Navy F-18 Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 dogs die from starvation, North Carolina man charged with animal cruelty
item.description: At the scene, police found the dog in question dead alongside another dead dog inside a small, enclosed fence with three other dogs.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's tries to reassure customers after deadly E. coli outbreak
item.description: McDonald’s said Wednesday that customers should feel confident ordering from its restaurants despite a deadly E. coli outbreak linked to its Quarter Pounder hamburgers.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DNA links a suspected Chicago-area serial killer who died in 1981 to a woman's 1979 death
item.description: NORTH AURORA, Ill. (AP) — DNA found on the clothing of a suburban Chicago woman who was kidnapped and killed 45 years ago matches that of a suspected serial killer who died two years later, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Kristy strengthens into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: Hurricane Kristy strengthened into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean on Wednesday and was expected to remain away from land as it churns in the Pacific Ocean, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Carville lists 3 reasons why he thinks Harris will win
item.description: “The biggest reason Mr. Trump will lose is that the whole Republican Party has been on a losing streak since Mr. Trump took it over,” he said in an essay published Wednesday by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Former Soros-backed St. Louis prosecutor admits to misusing public funds
item.description: Former St. Louis prosecutor Kimberly Gardner, who stepped down from her role in 2023, admitted to misusing public funds to cover her personal court costs and fees in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: They made 36 million Corn Poppers. Here's how they pick the right ones for the Toy Hall of Fame
item.description: ROCHESTER, N.Y. (AP) — When curators at the National Toy Hall of Fame learned last fall that the Fisher-Price Corn Popper had been voted in as part of the class of 2023, they knew they had some serious work to do.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video: Suspected drunken driver passes within feet of Harris' motorcade in Wisconsin
item.description: The driver was traveling in the wrong direction on the interstate in Milwaukee when deputies spotted him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump praised 'Hitler's generals': former chief of staff says
item.description: Retired Gen. John Kelly, who served as White House chief of staff in the Trump administration, said former President Trump praised Adolf Hitler's generals for their loyalty in a new interview in The Atlantic on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KTLA 5

item.title: Suspected dog-fighting ring busted; shelter director bitten
item.description: "We are struggling right now each of us individually and as a crew. The challenges are insurmountable," said the animal shelter in a social media post.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: Harvey Weinstein back in New York court for hearing ahead of retrial in sex crime case
item.description: Harvey Weinstein is back in court in New York for a hearing ahead of his retrial on rape charges stemming from his landmark #MeToo case, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump slams 'jerk' Obama as former president campaigns for Harris
item.description: The former president remains especially popular among Democrats, and he has used his appearances to try and appeal directly to Black men and to make the case that Trump is unfit to lead.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: LeBron and Bronny James make history as the NBA's first father-son duo to play together
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James gave his 20-year-old son a pep talk before they rose from the Lakers bench. Amid rising cheers, they walked together to the scorer's table — and then they stepped straight into basketball history.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: Disgraced Hollywood producer Harvey Weinstein diagnosed with bone cancer: Reports
item.description: Disgraced movie producer Harvey Weinstein has been diagnosed with bone cancer, according to reports. The infamous Hollywood mogul’s diagnosis of chronic myeloid leukemia was first reported by NBC News on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: In battleground Georgia, some poor people see no reason to vote. That decision could sway election
item.description: MACON, Ga. (AP) — Sabrina Friday scanned the room at Mother's Nest, an organization in Macon that provides baby supplies, training, food and housing to mothers in need, and she asked how many planned to vote. Of the 30, mostly women, six raised their hands.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans head to court in expanding battle about overseas ballots
item.description: Overseas voting has become the latest battlefront in Republicans’ legal challenges leading up to the election.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Woman needing double-lung transplant marries her donor's brother
item.description: One of the scariest moments turned into something more beautiful than one woman could've ever imagined.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: It's more dangerous to drive at night in these states than anywhere else in the US
item.description: Driving at night is dangerous no matter where you are in the U.S. It's harder to see and you're more likely to be sharing the road with fellow drivers who are either tired and could potentially doze off or have had one too many drinks and are driving under the influence.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WGN 9
